audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's final response and narrative ending: Job's humbled reply, hearing/seeing shift, contested 'repent' phrasing rendered as retract/relent in dust and ashes, divine rebuke of friends for not speaking rightly, Job's intercession, restoration, daughters named, and Job's death full of days.

verses.restored: Job answered the Eternal and said:
I know that you can do all things, and no purpose of yours can be cut off.
Who is this hiding counsel without knowledge? Therefore I declared what I did not understand, wonders too wonderful for me, which I did not know.
Hear, please, and I will speak; I will ask you, and you make it known to me.
By the hearing of the ear I had heard of you, but now my eye sees you.
Therefore I retract/relent, and I am comforted upon dust and ashes.

After the Eternal had spoken these words to Job, the Eternal said to Eliphaz the Temanite, My anger burns against you and against your two friends, because you have not spoken rightly of me as my servant Job has.

Now take for yourselves seven bulls and seven rams, go to my servant Job, and offer a burnt offering for yourselves. Job my servant will pray for you, for I will lift up his face, lest I deal with you according to your folly, because you have not spoken rightly of me as my servant Job has.
So Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as the Eternal had spoken to them, and the Eternal lifted up Job's face.
The Eternal turned the captivity/fortune of Job when he prayed for his friends, and the Eternal gave Job twice as much as he had before.

All his brothers, all his sisters, and all who had known him before came to him. They ate bread with him in his house, showed sympathy, and comforted him for all the harm the Eternal had brought upon him. Each gave him one piece of silver and one gold ring.
The Eternal blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning. He had fourteen thousand sheep, six thousand camels, one thousand yoke of oxen, and one thousand female donkeys.
He also had seven sons and three daughters.
He called the name of the first Jemimah, the name of the second Keziah, and the name of the third Keren-happuch.
In all the land no women were found as beautiful as Job's daughters, and their father gave them inheritance among their brothers.
After this Job lived one hundred forty years and saw his sons and his sons' sons, four generations.
Job died old and full of days.
